parentQuestion: During childbirth, cervical stretch triggers oxytocin release, which intensifies...

scaffolds.q: Positive feedback amplifies the original stimulus rather than opposing it.
scaffolds.explain: Unlike negative feedback (which opposes change), positive feedback ENHANCES the original stimulus. Output feeds back to intensify input. It's a self-accelerating loop — explosive, dramatic, requiring an OFF switch. Surrender to the logic here:drawn into this.

scaffolds.q: Most physiological processes use negative feedback to maintain stability.
scaffolds.explain: About 99% of physiological control systems use NEGATIVE feedback because it's self-limiting and maintains homeostasis. Positive feedback is RARE — reserved for processes that need to happen FAST and COMPLETELY. you're about to see why that distinction is life-or-death.
